--- title: "NAC GP Modeling" jupyter: python3 --- ```{=html} ``` # Setup This report picks up where `nac.qmd`'s Analysis Region / OHC Anomaly sections leave off, without re-running any of that report's plotting or re-deriving its climatology. `nac.qmd` publishes the ANALYSIS_BOUNDS-restricted, multi-year truth field and the truth-vs-climatology anomaly cells to `data/ohc_truth/`; loading those directly (~1s) skips both the expensive depth-integration step (cached separately, per year, as `data/ohc_truth/truth_ohc_.nc`) and the RG climatology reprocessing, neither of which this report needs to repeat. ::: {#setup .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Setup — imports and experiment configuration"} import sys, os sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath("../code")) import numpy as np import pandas as pd import xarray as xr import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import ohc import ohc_bias import ohc_climatology as ohc_clim YEARS = [2020, 2021, 2022] ANALYSIS_BOUNDS = (36, 40, -68, -62) EXTENT_WIDE = [-76, -47, 28, 50] OHC_TRUTH_DIR = "../data/ohc_truth" DEPTHS = ["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"] ``` ::: ::: {#load-cached-data .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Load nac.qmd's published truth field and truth-vs-climatology anomaly"} truth_field_all = xr.open_dataset(f"{OHC_TRUTH_DIR}/truth_field_analysis.nc").load() truth_anom_cells_all = pd.read_csv(f"{OHC_TRUTH_DIR}/truth_anom_cells_all.csv", parse_dates=["month"]) ``` ::: # Real Argo Float Trajectories Real Argo floats that profiled the analysis domain at any point during 2020-2022, from `data/argo_ohc.csv`. ::: {#fig-real-argo-traj .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} from trajplots import map_point_trajectories la0, la1, lo0, lo1 = ANALYSIS_BOUNDS ids = argo_all.loc[ argo_all.lat.between(la0, la1) & argo_all.lon.between(lo0, lo1) & argo_all["date"].dt.year.isin(YEARS), "float_id", ].unique() argo_a = argo_all[argo_all.float_id.isin(ids)] print(f"{len(ids)} floats entered the analysis domain during {YEARS[0]}-{YEARS[-1]}") _ = map_point_trajectories( argo_a, extent=EXTENT_WIDE, boxes=[(*ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, "analysis domain")], title=f"All obs for floats that profiled the domain, {YEARS[0]}-{YEARS[-1]}", ) # Restrict to the analysis window and box itself -- this is the profile set # the GP interpolation below is fit on. (The year filter alone isn't enough: # a float that entered the box can spend most of a year drifting outside it, # and those out-of-box obs would otherwise get carried through as phantom # rows that silently turn to NaN once truth is sampled at their position -- # quietly dropped from the fit, but only after wasting the sampling/interp # work on ~8x too many rows.) argo_a = argo_a[ argo_a["date"].dt.year.isin(YEARS) & argo_a.lat.between(la0, la1) & argo_a.lon.between(lo0, lo1) ] _ = map_point_trajectories( argo_a, extent=EXTENT_WIDE, boxes=[(*ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, "analysis domain")], title=f"{YEARS[0]}-{YEARS[-1]} obs within the analysis domain", ) print(f"{len(argo_a)} profiles within the analysis domain, {YEARS[0]}-{YEARS[-1]}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` 40 floats entered the analysis domain during 2020-2022 ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![Real Argo floats, 2020-2022](nac_gp_files/figure-html/fig-real-argo-traj-output-2.png){#fig-real-argo-traj-1 width=726 height=566} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](nac_gp_files/figure-html/fig-real-argo-traj-output-3.png){#fig-real-argo-traj-2 width=726 height=566} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` 364 profiles within the analysis domain, 2020-2022 ``` ::: ::: # GP Interpolation with Real Floats ## Background We interpolate real Argo float OHC (`ohc_700`/`ohc_2000` directly, not their anomaly relative to climatology) onto the full analysis-domain grid at the native 1/12$^\circ$ resolution, using a Gaussian process fit with `GpGp`. Each real float profile is assigned the **GLORYS truth OHC** at its position and date. `code/ohc_gp_fit.R` fits one GP per depth (0-700m, 0-2000m) with the stationary spatiotemporal covariance function `matern_spheretime`, pooling all profiles across all three years, with covariance parameters $\theta$ = ($\sigma^2$, $\rho_s$, $\rho_t$, $\nu$, $\tau^2$) = (variance, spatial range, temporal range, smoothness, nugget). Smoothness is fixed at $\nu = \tfrac{1}{2}$, where the Matérn form reduces to the simple exponential $k(r) = e^{-r}$. The temporal coordinate is a continuous day count (days since 2020-01-01), not day-of-year -- so the fitted temporal range can extend across years rather than being capped at within-year scale by an annual wraparound, letting the model express long-range temporal autocorrelation if the data support it. Fitting and prediction are separate scripts, so one fit is reused across both prediction grids below. `code/ohc_gp_predict.R` predicts via a custom Vecchia kriging step (`kriging_predict`) that gives both a mean and a standard error -- `GpGp::predictions()` alone is faster but has no SE. Predicting on the coarse 1° grid (35 points) conditions each point on every one of the ~3,450 pooled profiles ("exact" mode) -- cheap at that grid size, and matches the reference. Predicting on the native 1/12° grid (~3,600 points x 36 months x 2 depths) with that same full conditioning does not scale -- so that pass instead uses a bounded Vecchia neighbourhood (`m = 30`, matching the fit's own approximation), which reuses the same underlying machinery but conditions each point on only its 30 nearest profiles. ## Model Fit ::: {#gp-real-prep .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Sample GLORYS truth OHC at each real Argo profile's position/date"} _dates = xr.DataArray(pd.to_datetime(argo_a["date"]).values, dims="profile") _lats = xr.DataArray(argo_a["lat"].values, dims="profile") _lons = xr.DataArray(argo_a["lon"].values, dims="profile") _truth_at_real = truth_field_all.interp(time=_dates, latitude=_lats, longitude=_lons, method="nearest") real_argo_direct = argo_a[["lat", "lon", "date"]].copy() for _d in DEPTHS: real_argo_direct[_d] = _truth_at_real[_d].values ``` ::: ::: {#tbl-gp-fit-diagnostics .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Fit the pooled GP once, then predict on the 1$^\circ$ (exact) and native 1/12$^\circ$ (bounded Vecchia) grids"} _GP_DIR = "../data/gp_interp" os.makedirs(_GP_DIR, exist_ok=True) _real_profiles_csv = f"{_GP_DIR}/nac_gp_profiles_real_direct.csv" _real_fit_summary_csv = f"{_GP_DIR}/nac_gp_real_direct_fit_summary.csv" _real_model_cache = f"{_GP_DIR}/nac_gp_real_direct_model_cache.rds" _keys = ["month", "cell_lat", "cell_lon"] _GP_MONTH_DAY = "middle" _GP_RES_LABELS = {1 / 12: "1/12$^\circ$ (native)", 1.0: "1$^\circ$"} # Full conditioning ("exact") is fine at 35 points; the ~3,600-point native # grid needs the bounded Vecchia approximation to stay fast -- see Background. _GP_PREDICT_M = {1 / 12: 30, 1.0: "exact"} _pred_grid_csvs = {deg: f"{_GP_DIR}/nac_gp_pred_grid_{deg:.4g}deg.csv" for deg in [1 / 12, 1.0]} _real_out_csvs = {deg: f"{_GP_DIR}/nac_gp_real_predictions_{deg:.4g}deg.csv" for deg in [1 / 12, 1.0]} def _grid_lats_lons(deg): """Prediction grid for one resolution -- native truth grid, or a deg-degree coarsening of it.""" if deg < 0.1: return truth_field_all["latitude"].values, truth_field_all["longitude"].values _tc = ohc.coarsen_truth(truth_field_all, deg=deg) return np.sort(_tc["cell_lat"].unique()), np.sort(_tc["cell_lon"].unique()) for deg, grid_csv in _pred_grid_csvs.items(): lats, lons = _grid_lats_lons(deg) ohc_clim.build_pred_grid(lats, lons).to_csv(grid_csv, index=False) ohc_clim.write_profile_csv(real_argo_direct, _real_profiles_csv, suffix="") ohc_clim.run_gp_fit(_real_profiles_csv, _real_fit_summary_csv, _real_model_cache) for deg, out_csv in _real_out_csvs.items(): ohc_clim.run_gp_predict( _real_profiles_csv, _pred_grid_csvs[deg], _real_model_cache, out_csv, m=_GP_PREDICT_M[deg], month_day=_GP_MONTH_DAY, ) gp_fit_summary = ohc_clim.load_gp_fit_summary(_real_fit_summary_csv) gp_fit_summary.groupby("depth")[["loglik", "converged", "n_obs"]].first() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` - The project is out-of-sync -- use `renv::status()` for details. ohc_700: pooled fit n=363, covparms=1.748e+20, 0.07912, 238.5, 0.5, 0.03434 ohc_2000: pooled fit n=363, covparms=4.502e+20, 0.08741, 235.3, 0.5, 0.003117 saved model cache to /storage/work/dmr6737/argo_bias/data/gp_interp/nac_gp_real_direct_model_cache.rds wrote 12 rows -> /storage/work/dmr6737/argo_bias/data/gp_interp/nac_gp_real_direct_fit_summary.csv - The project is out-of-sync -- use `renv::status()` for details. wrote 128772 rows -> /storage/work/dmr6737/argo_bias/data/gp_interp/nac_gp_real_predictions_0.08333deg.csv - The project is out-of-sync -- use `renv::status()` for details. wrote 1260 rows -> /storage/work/dmr6737/argo_bias/data/gp_interp/nac_gp_real_predictions_1deg.csv ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=7} ```{=html}
loglik converged n_obs
depth
ohc_2000 -8831.233067 True 363
ohc_700 -8709.519910 True 363
``` ::: ::: ::: {#gp-interp-prep .cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Load the GP-predicted OHC field directly (no climatology to add back)"} def build_gp_res_ctx(out_csv, deg, profiles_df=None): """Load GP predictions from out_csv and assemble the display context for resolution deg. Modeling OHC directly (suffix="" throughout) means the GP's prediction *is* the field -- no per-year climatology reassembly needed here, unlike the anomaly-modeling case. """ lats, lons = _grid_lats_lons(deg) gp_field = ohc_clim.load_gp_anomaly_field(out_csv, lats, lons, suffix="") cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(gp_field) se_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(gp_field, value_cols=("ohc_700_se", "ohc_2000_se")) obs_cells = ( ohc.grid_cells(profiles_df, list(DEPTHS), deg=1 / 12) if profiles_df is not None else None ) if deg < 0.1: truth_monthly_all = truth_field_all.resample(time="1MS").mean().rename({"time": "month"}) _bf = (gp_field[list(DEPTHS)] - truth_monthly_all[list(DEPTHS)]).rename( {d: f"{d}_bias" for d in DEPTHS} ) bias_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(_bf, value_cols=[f"{d}_bias" for d in DEPTHS]) truth_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(truth_monthly_all) else: # coarsen_truth resamples+bins by each row's own date, so one call over # all three concatenated years already yields distinct per-year cells. truth_by_deg_all = ohc.coarsen_truth(truth_field_all, deg=deg) _merged = cells.merge( truth_by_deg_all[_keys + list(DEPTHS)].rename(columns={d: f"{d}_truth" for d in DEPTHS}), on=_keys, how="left", ) bias_cells = _merged.copy() for _d in DEPTHS: bias_cells[f"{_d}_bias"] = bias_cells[_d] - bias_cells[f"{_d}_truth"] truth_cells = truth_by_deg_all return dict( cells=cells, se_cells=se_cells, obs_cells=obs_cells, bias_cells=bias_cells, truth_cells=truth_cells, obs_profiles=profiles_df, ) def build_gp_ctx(out_csvs, profiles_df=None): return {_GP_RES_LABELS[deg]: build_gp_res_ctx(out_csv, deg, profiles_df) for deg, out_csv in out_csvs.items()} _real_ctx = build_gp_ctx(_real_out_csvs, profiles_df=real_argo_direct) ``` ::: ::: {#tbl-gp-fit-summary .cell execution_count=9} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Format the pooled GP fit parameter table for display"} def _fmt_fit_summary(df): d = df.copy() d["display"] = d.apply( lambda r: f"{r['estimate']:.4g}" if pd.isna(r["std_error"]) else f"{r['estimate']:.4g} $\pm$ {r['std_error']:.3g}", axis=1, ) wide = d.pivot(index="parameter", columns="depth", values="display") order = ["mean (intercept)", "variance", "spatial_range", "temporal_range", "smoothness", "nugget"] return wide.reindex([p for p in order if p in wide.index]) EARTH_RADIUS_KM = 6371.0 _scale = { "mean (intercept)": ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, "variance": ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ ** 2, "spatial_range": EARTH_RADIUS_KM, "temporal_range": 1.0, "smoothness": 1.0, "nugget": 1.0, } _disp = gp_fit_summary.copy() _disp["estimate"] = _disp.apply(lambda r: r["estimate"] * _scale[r["parameter"]], axis=1) _disp["std_error"] = _disp.apply(lambda r: r["std_error"] * _scale[r["parameter"]], axis=1) fit_table = _fmt_fit_summary(_disp) fit_table.index.name = "parameter" fit_table ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=9} ```{=html}
depth ohc_2000 ohc_700
parameter
mean (intercept) 70.58 $\pm$ 9.68 43.29 $\pm$ 5.99
variance 450.2 $\pm$ 184 174.8 $\pm$ 70.1
spatial_range 556.9 $\pm$ 248 504.1 $\pm$ 230
temporal_range 235.3 $\pm$ 105 238.5 $\pm$ 109
smoothness 0.5 0.5
nugget 0.003117 $\pm$ 0.00831 0.03434 $\pm$ 0.0212
``` ::: ::: **Units** mean (GJ m$^{{-2}}$), variance (GJ m$^{{-4}}$), spatial_range (km), temporal_range (days), nugget (relative fraction of variance) ## Predictions ::: {#gp-interp-maps .cell execution_count=11} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="GP-interpolated field vs native-grid truth, its standard error, and the GP - truth bias map"} def make_gp_map(c, kind, d, res_label): if kind == "obs": _lats, _lons = _grid_lats_lons(1 / 12) return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps( c["obs_cells"], d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], scatter_dots=True, width=TABSET_FIGW, lats=_lats, lons=_lons, title=f"Observed Argo {d} by month ({res_label})") if kind == "gp": return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps( c["cells"], d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW, title=f"GP-interpolated {d} by month ({res_label})") if kind == "gp_se": return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps( c["se_cells"], f"{d}_se", vmin=0, cmap="viridis", width=TABSET_FIGW, cbar_label=f"{d} GP standard error (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", title=f"GP standard error, {d} ({res_label})") if kind == "truth_native": return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps( c["truth_cells"], d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW, title=f"GLORYS truth {d} by month ({res_label})") if kind == "gp_bias": bcol = f"{d}_bias" bmax = float(c["bias_cells"][bcol].abs().max()) * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps( c["bias_cells"], bcol, vmin=-bmax, vmax=bmax, cmap="RdBu_r", cbar_label=f"{d} bias (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", width=TABSET_FIGW, title=f"GP - truth bias, {d} ({res_label})") GP_MAP_KINDS = [("obs", "Observed Argo OHC"), ("obs_table", "Observed Argo OHC table"), ("gp", "GP-interpolated Argo OHC"), ("gp_se", "GP standard error"), ("truth_native", "GLORYS truth"), ("gp_bias", "GP - truth bias")] def make_gp_table(c, d): profiles = c.get("obs_profiles") if profiles is None: return None col = d s = profiles[col] grp = profiles.groupby(pd.to_datetime(profiles["date"]).dt.to_period("M"))[col] raw = grp.agg(["count", "mean", "std", "min", "max"]) tbl = raw.assign( mean=(raw["mean"] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).round(3), std=(raw["std"] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).round(3), min=(raw["min"] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).round(3), max=(raw["max"] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).round(3), ).rename(columns={"count": "n", "std": "sd"}) tbl.index = tbl.index.astype(str) tbl.index.name = "month" year_row = pd.DataFrame([{ "n": s.count(), "mean": round(s.mean() * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, 3), "sd": round(s.std() * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, 3), "min": round(s.min() * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, 3), "max": round(s.max() * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, 3), }], index=pd.Index(["Year"], name="month")) return pd.concat([tbl, year_row]) def emit_gp_map_tabset(ctx): _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}") for d in DEPTHS: _emit(f"## {DEPTH_LABEL[d]}") _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}") for res_label, c in ctx.items(): _emit(f"### {res_label}") _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}") for kind, label in GP_MAP_KINDS: _emit(f"#### {label}") if kind == "obs_table": tbl = make_gp_table(c, d) if tbl is not None: display(tbl) else: _show_fig(make_gp_map(c, kind, d, res_label)) _emit(":::") _emit(":::") _emit(":::") emit_gp_map_tabset(_real_ctx) ``` ::: {.panel-tabset} ## 0–700 m ::: {.panel-tabset} ### 1/12$^\circ$ (native) ::: {.panel-tabset} #### Observed Argo OHC
![](nac_gp_files/figure-html/gp-interp-maps-output-2.png){#gp-interp-maps-1 width=639 height=1548}
